task_completion.description: Select or filter the Zotero library for items tagged 'cite-in-paper'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Zotero window showing the 'cite-in-paper' tag selected in the bottom-left tag selector or search bar, displaying the list of target items.

task_completion.description: Fix the missing publication/journal names for the 3 specific items
task_completion.visual_evidence: Zotero's right-hand info pane showing the agent editing or filling in the 'Publication' field for 'Algebraic Complexity Theory and Circuit Lower Bounds', 'Interactive Proofs and Zero-Knowledge Arguments', or 'Parameterized Complexity and Kernelization'.

task_completion.description: Resolve the duplicate entries in the library
task_completion.visual_evidence: Agent interacting with the 'Duplicate Items' view in Zotero's left pane and clicking 'Merge', or manually deleting the duplicate entries for 'Linear Programming and Extensions', 'Computability and Unsolvability', and 'Foundations of Cryptography: Basic Tools'.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export process for the corrected items in BibTeX format
task_completion.visual_evidence: Zotero 'Export...' dialog box open with the format dropdown set to 'BibTeX'.

task_completion.description: Save the exported BibTeX file to the specified Desktop path
task_completion.visual_evidence: File save dialog showing the directory '/home/ga/Desktop' (or 'Desktop') and the filename 'references.bib', or a terminal/file manager confirming the file exists at this exact location.

integrity.description: The agent used the Zotero graphical interface to fix the items rather than modifying the underlying SQLite database directly via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the Zotero application window (clicking, typing in fields).

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type or copy-paste the BibTeX entries into a text editor to bypass Zotero's export function.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) being used to manually construct the 'references.bib' file from scratch.

integrity.description: The final file was generated via Zotero's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Zotero export dialog and subsequent save dialog are visible in the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target items are tagged 'cite-in-paper'. There are exactly 3 items with missing journal titles that must be fixed: 'Algebraic Complexity Theory and Circuit Lower Bounds', 'Interactive Proofs and Zero-Knowledge Arguments', and 'Parameterized Complexity and Kernelization'. There are exactly 3 duplicate pairs (6 items total) that need deduplication: 'Linear Programming and Extensions', 'Computability and Unsolvability', and 'Foundations of Cryptography: Basic Tools'. The final exported file must be saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/references.bib' and should contain exactly 17 entries. Note: Some papers in the database have intentionally inaccurate metadata (e.g., wrong authors), but the agent is ONLY required to fix the missing publication names and duplicates as specified in the task.
